text: I find myself genuinely charmed by the texture of the pages in very old books, that particular combination of softness and slight brittleness, the way the paper feels almost cottony compared to modern pages, with a faint powdery quality at the edges where time has done its work. There is something wonderful about how it yields slightly under your fingertips while still holding its form, and how running your thumb across the corner of a stack of those pages produces a sound that feels proportionate to the texture, a soft, muffled flutter rather than a crisp snap. It is the kind of thing that makes you slow down and pay attention.
